item.title: 您是否见过有人“冒充”进入软件开发职位？ | Have you ever seen anyone "fake" their way into a software development position?
item.description:
雪利酒日

发表于有没有人通过面试，被录用，但严重无法完成实际工作？

一旦暂停，sherrydays 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，sherrydays 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，sherrydays 发布的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 sherrydays 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记雪利酒的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记的雪利酒将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Sherry Day

Posted on Oct 6

Are there instances of people who make it through the interview, get hired, but seriously can't do the actual job?

Once suspended, sherrydays will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, sherrydays will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by sherrydays will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If sherrydays is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag sherrydays:

sherrydays consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging sherrydays will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: sherrydays

item.title: 作为 React 开发人员，你应该知道的 16 个库💯🔥 | 16 Libraries You Should Know as a React Developer 💯🔥
item.description:
作为一名现代的 React 开发人员，不仅仅是了解 React 本身。 为了保持竞争力，强烈建议探索整个生态系统。

在本文中，我编译了一些最有用的 React 组件库，您可以使用它们来加快开发人员的工作流程。

这些将包括使用表格、图表、日历、表格、指南、弹出窗口、颜色、动画、音乐、图像等等。

用于表单状态管理和验证的 React Hooks。

重新定义了使用 React 和 D3 构建的图表库。

为 React 和现代浏览器构建的事件日历。

使用 React 进行列表的漂亮且易于访问的拖放操作。

用于构建强大的表格和数据网格的库。

为您的应用创建导览。

为您的设计创建定制作物。

一个小巧、无依赖、快速且可访问的颜色选择器组件。

用于 React 应用程序的基于 Spring-physics 的动画库。

轻松创建高度可定制的粒子动画。

以优雅、高效的方式定位工具提示和弹出框。

为 React 制作的 PDF 查看器。

具有自定义控件、播放列表、过滤器和搜索功能的音乐播放器。

图片的图像滑块组件。

写作一直是我的热情所在，它让我乐于帮助和激励人们。 如果您有任何问题，请随时与我们联系！

在Twitter 、 LinkedIn和GitHub 上联系我！

访问我的博客以获取更多类似的文章。



原文:

Being a modern React developer is not about knowing just React itself. To stay competitive, it is highly recommended to explore the whole ecosystem.

In this article I've compiled some of the most useful React component libraries that you could use to speed up your developer workflow.

Those will include anything from working with forms, charts, calendars, tables, guides, popups, colors, animations, music, images and so much more.

1. react-hook-form

React Hooks for form state management and validation.

2. recharts

Redefined chart library built with React and D3.

3. react-big-calendar

An events calendar built for React and modern browsers.

4. react-beautiful-dnd

Beautiful and accessible drag and drop for lists with React.

5. react-table

A library for building powerful tables and data grids.

6. react-joyride

Create guided tours for your apps.

7. react-advanced-cropper

Create customized crops for your designs.

8. react-colorful

A tiny, dependency-free, fast and accessible color picker component.

9. react-spring

Spring-physics based animation library for React applications.

10. react-tsparticles

Easily create highly customizable particles animations.

11. react-popper

Position tooltips and popovers in an elegant, performant manner.

12. react-pdf-viewer

A PDF viewer made for React.

13. react-i18next

Internationalization for React done right.

14. react-icons

SVG react icons of popular icon packs.

15. audio-player

Music player with custom controls, playlist, filters, and search.

16. image-slider

Image slider component for your pictures.

Writing has always been my passion and it gives me pleasure to help and inspire people. If you have any questions, feel free to reach out!

Connect me on Twitter, LinkedIn and GitHub!

Visit my Blog for more articles like this.
item.author: madza

item.title: 👷 使用 AWS 进行无服务器事件溯源：最先进的数据同步 ⚡ | 👷 Serverless event-sourcing with AWS: State of the art data synchronization ⚡
item.description:
如果您要为基于事件溯源的应用程序实施无服务器架构，那么您来对地方了。 这篇文章旨在和大家分享2年的心得😍，免去我们在Kumo犯过同样错误的烦恼，并为您提供一本指南，让您拥有最稳定、最不痛苦的解决方案。 如果您在深入研究有趣的事物之前觉得有必要，这篇文章是一个很好的页面，可以更深入地了解 CQRS 模式的来龙去脉。

这些业务事件是在银行帐户上进行的交易，例如贷记和借记操作。

让我们以需要显示账户余额和最后一次交易的银行应用程序为例。

该架构是银行用例的CQRS 接口的实现，该用例必须存储所有贷记和借记事件。 有两个命令在事件分类帐中写入贷方和借方事件。 有两个查询，每个查询都获取其关联的projection ，即帐户的总余额和最后一次进行的交易。

如您所见，图中缺少的部分是写模型和读模型之间的同步。 这就是本文关注的块。

每个用户的所有贷记和借记事件都存储在事件分类帐中。 选择的访问模式是userId作为分区键，事件的编号也称为version作为排序键。

同时传入的两个命令将基于相同版本的聚合写入数据，因为它们同时获取它。 假设一个用户在他的银行账户上有 50 美元，并且同时进行了两次购买，一次是 40 美元，一次是 25 美元。 这两个事件都将成功通过业务验证测试。 两个事件的复合键userId / timestamp将是唯一的，因为时间戳将非常接近但不完全相同。 因此，这两个事件都将写入事件分类帐中，您将被透支，这在我们的银行中是禁止的。

另一方面，如果排序键是事件的version ，则两者中的一个将是 invalid 。 计算机科学中不存在精确的同时性，因此将写入 40 美元的第一次购买，然后在第二次购买后不久将被拒绝，因为要写入的事件基于上次事件的相同版本。

预测需要根据事件分类帐中写入的事件进行更新。

这里的目标是实时响应事件分类帐中写入的事件，以计算预测。 为此，可以通过将 lambda 插入事件分类帐的DynamoDB 流来使用作为扇出的 lambda。 必须使用过滤器模式过滤INSERT操作，以便只有这些操作触发扇出。 这些 EventBridge 事件是那些触发投射到读取模型上的 lambda 的事件。

触发失败 lambda 的流事件将无限期重试，直到 lambda 成功。 这使得无法为触发投影仪的事件制定自定义重试策略。

  • 您最多只能将2 个 lambda插入流中，并且通常应用程序包含超过 2 组要显示的数据，因此超过 2 个投影仪。
  • 将多个侦听器插入消息的正确工具是事件总线。

    • 扇出将事件传递给一个或多个接收者。 作为这些事件的接收者的每台投影仪只需要监听改变其关联数据的事件。 例如，写入用户个人信息（如姓名和地址）的投影仪不需要由信用事件触发。

    这在开发中运行良好，但在生产中还不行。 这种数据同步架构稍后将面临错误。 让我解释 ：

    • 当流量开始增长时，将会有越来越多的事件流动，而EventBridge 无法确保的一件事是总线事件中发送的事件的顺序。 为了不使用较旧的信息覆盖信息，必须跟踪更新投影的事件的最后版本。
    • 所以预测需要是幂等的。
    • 在开发中，当需要更新投影或需要创建新投影时，擦除数据库并创建新数据是测试这一新功能的简单方法。 但在生产中，这不是一个选择。 预测是从发生的所有事件的连续性中做出的，这就是为什么解决方案是找到一种方法来重放所有事件以重新创建预测。 投影仪必须具有幂等性和无序性，因此它们可以顺利处理所有事件的重播。

    此功能称为EventBridge 存档。

    该解决方案存在 3 个主要问题：

    • 当有大量事件时，重播所有事件意味着高流量。
    • 重播的某些事件可能是无用的，因此这是无用的流量，因此是无用的运行时间。
    • 这留下了两个真相来源。 而且存档数据不能编辑，它是不可变的。 这可能是一个问题。

    最好的解决方案是，每台投影机每次需要更新投影时都可以访问数据的当前状态。 每次需要更新投影时，他们都必须获取所有事件来计算聚合。 将同时跨多个投影仪计算相同的聚合，这使得该解决方案效率不高。

    我们在 Kumo 提出的技巧是实现状态承载事件。 不是在每个投影仪中计算相同的聚合并让它们都访问事件分类帐，而是计算一次聚合并将其附加到调度的事件。 它采用新的 Lambda 函数的形式，该函数仅接受一个事件作为参数，计算聚合并将其附加到事件中，然后再重新发布。 我们将其命名为dispatchAggregate 。

    扇出仍将流数组转换为触发 dispatchAggregate Lambda 的已发布 EventBridge 事件。 通过不添加从所有 DynamoDB 事件中计算聚合的操作，它的失败风险仍然非常低。

    这是 EventBridge 事件，例如它在扇出之后调度，例如它在技巧之前由投影仪侦听。

     { "userId" : "163082d2-b005-75d7-5eb6-aa1f83efd55d" , "eventType" : "Debit" , "payload" : { "amount" : 30 }, "version" : 3
}

    这是触发包含聚合的投影仪的事件的形状。

     { "aggregate" : { "lastKnownVersion" : 3 , "totalBalance" : 500 , "lastMonthBalance" : -20 }, "lastEvent" : { "userId" : "163082d2-b005-75d7-5eb6-aa1f83efd55d" , "eventType" : "Debit" , "payload" : { "amount" : 30 }, "version" : 3 }
}

    聚合表示所考虑的最新事件版本中数据的当前状态。 此外，聚合的形状没有规则，可以以最可利用的方式进行定制。 对于这个用例，它可以用键作为所有投影来构建。

     { "lastKnownVersion" : 3 , "totalBalance" : 500 , "lastMonthBalance" : -20
}

    通过访问聚合，投影仪可以随意使用他们每个处理的投影的确切值以及该数据的最新版本。 因此不再需要根据先前的值更新投影。 只有当传入的版本严格优于当前存储的版本时，投影仪才可以简单地使用 DynamoDB 的PutItem命令覆盖先前的值。 预测变得幂等，并且它们在触发方式上允许更大的灵活性。

    这就像在分类帐中编写replay类型事件一样简单。 所有投影仪都需要监听对应的 EventBridge 事件。 然后，每次在分类帐中写入replay类型的事件时，所有投影仪都会使用其最新版本更新其投影。

    但在检查了我们在 lambda 上的结果后，这是可以接受的。

    冷启动的 dispatchAggregate lambda 的运行时间平均为250 毫秒，并且几乎一致。

    但是，当它已经很热时，相同 lambda 的运行时间对于最慢的运行在30 毫秒到80毫秒之间，具体取决于所讨论的用户userId 。

    延迟通常非常低，因为事件只有一个网关通过，因此 lambda 大部分时间都很热。

    当事件数量变得巨大时，运行时主题可能会成为一个问题。

    为了计算聚合，必须在 lambda 中获取和减少所有事件，处理这么多事件可能真的很长。 但在这里，有一个解决方案。 事实上，聚合的快​​照可以存储在事件分类账中。 表示版本 N 的聚合的快照允许不担心版本严格低于 N 的事件，并在计算聚合时从该版本开始。

    自 2022 年夏天以来，我提到的关于插入流的 lambda 的重试策略以及可以插入流的 lambda 数量的问题都不再适用。

    在效率方面，我们仍然需要单个 lambda来计算聚合，因为它是耗时的操作，因此将多个 lambda 插入流的规则不会改变这种架构。

    这里的机会是我们可以让 lambda 将事件分派给失败的投影仪，而不必担心无限重试或丢失数据。 错误处理允许为失败事件和我选择的重试策略配置目标。 同时拥有一个不能失败的扇出和一个 dispatchAggregate 来计算聚合并将聚合附加到事件变得无用。 现在可以跳过一个步骤，方法是让dispatchAggregate 接收 DynamoDB 流、计算聚合并将它们附加到事件中，然后再将它们发布到 EventBridge 中。 必须在此 lambda 上配置重试策略和失败事件的目标。

    这应该使其成为在事件溯源应用程序中同步事件分类帐和预测之间的数据的最有效和最新的解决方案。

    我们将所有这些学习集中在一个库中： Castore



    原文:

    If you're about to implement a serverless architecture for an event sourcing based application, you are in the right place. This article aims at sharing with you 2 years of learnings 😍, saving you the trouble to make the same mistakes we did at Kumo, and providing you with a guidebook to have the most stable and the least painful solution you can go with. 🚀

    📚 In this article I assume that you have at least a basic knowledge of what is CQRS. This article is an excellent page to understand more deeply the ins and outs of CQRS pattern if you feel the need before diving into the interesting things.

    👀 Quick overview of an event sourcing solution implementing a CQRS interface

    💸 Bank applications are one type of business requiring to store a trace of all business events. These business events are the transactions made on a bank account such as credit and debit actions.

    Let's take the example of a banking application that would need to display both the balance of the account and the last transaction made.

    This architecture is an implementation of CQRS interface for the use case of the bank which has to store all credit and debit events. There are two commands writing credit and debit events in an event ledger. There are two queries that each fetch its associated projection, the total balance of the account and the last transaction made.

    As you can see, the missing part of the diagram is the synchronization between the write model and the read model. That's the block this article focus on.

    📊 First and foremost, in event sourcing, the order of events is essential

    All credit and debit events for every user are stored in the event ledger. The access pattern chosen is the userId as the partition key and the number of the event also called the version as the sort key.

    ❓ Why using a version field when we can simply take the timestamp to keep track of the order of the events?

    Two commands coming in concurrently will write data based on the same version of the aggregate because they fetched it simultaneously. Let's say a user has 50$ on his bank account and two purchases come in at the same time, one of 40$ and one of 25$. Both event would both succeed the business validation tests. The composite key userId/timestamp of both event would be unique because timestamps would be really close but not exactly the same. Hence, both events would be written in the event ledger and you would be overdrawn which is forbidden in our bank.

    On the other hand, if the sort key is the version of the event, one out of both would be invalid. Exact simultaneity doesn't exist in computer science, so the first purchase of 40$ would be written and then shortly after the second purchase would be rejected because the event to be written is based on the same version of the last event.

    👉 👈 How is the synchronization between read and write models ensured ?

    The pain point in CQRS is to have reliable reading data compared to the source of truth. Projections need to be updated according to events written in the event ledger.

    👂 Listening to written events

    The objective here is to react in real time to events written inside the event ledger in order to compute the projections. For that a lambda as a fanout can be used by plugging it to the DynamoDB streams of the event ledger. INSERT actions have to be filtered with the filter patterns so that only theses actions trigger the fanout. DynamoDB batches its streams of events in arrays, so for each of them an event is published in Amazon EventBridge. These EventBridge events are those triggering the lambda that project on the read models.

    ❓ You may wonder why not directly plug the projector to the streams

    For 3 reasons:

    • Stream events that trigger a failing lambda will retry indefinitely until the lambda succeeds. That makes it impossible to have a custom retry policy for events triggering a projector.
    • You can only plug 2 lambda maximum to the streams, and generally application contain more than 2 sets of data to display therefore more than 2 projectors.
    • The correct tool to plug multiple listeners to messages is an Event Bus.

    The fanout delivers events to one or multiple recipients. Each projector, which is a recipient of those events, only needs to listen to events altering the data it's associated to. For example a projector writing user individual information such as the name family name and address doesn't need to be triggered by a credit event.

    💪 From a dev reliable architecture to a prod-safe architecture

    This works fine in dev but not in production yet. This data synchronization architecture will face bugs later on. Let me explain :

    • When traffic starts to grow, more and more events will flow and one thing EventBridge doesn't ensure is the order of the events sent in the bus event. In order not to overwrite an information with an older one, the last version of event that updated the projection must be kept track of.
    • EventBridge ensures that an event is sent at least once, but it can still be received more than once. So projections need to be idempotent.
    • In dev, when a projection needs to be updated or a new one needs to be created, wiping the databases and creating new data is the easy way to go to test this new feature. But in production that is not an option. Projections are made from the succession of all events that happened, that's why the solution is to find a way to replay all events to recreate the projections. Projectors must be idempotent and disorder-proof, thus they can handle smoothly a replay of all events.

    😒 EventBridge Archive, the bogus good idea

    💡 EventBridge offers the possibility to replay all events that went through its service. This feature is called EventBridge Archive.

    There are 3 main issues with that solution:

    • Replaying all events implies a high traffic when having a high number of events.
    • Some events that are replayed can be useless, so that's useless traffic and thus useless run time.
    • This leaves two sources of truth. And Archive data can't be edited, it is immutable. It can be an issue.

    🪄 A trick to make data synchronization and replay easier

    The best solution would be that every projector has access to the current state of the data every time they need to update their projection. They would all have to fetch all events to compute the aggregate every time an update of the projection would be needed. The same aggregate would be computed across multiple projectors at the same time, which makes this solution not very efficient.

    😎 Unless... !

    The trick we came up with at Kumo is implementing state carried events. Instead of computing the same aggregate in every projector and giving them all access to the event ledger, the aggregate is computed once and it is attached to the dispatched events. It takes the form of a new Lambda function that takes only one event in argument, computes the aggregate and attaches it to the event before republishing it. We named it dispatchAggregate.

    The fanout still transforms an array of streams to published EventBridge events triggering the dispatchAggregate Lambda. It also has still very low risk of failing by not adding the action of computing the aggregate out of all DynamoDB events.

    This is the EventBridge event such as it is dispatched after the fanout and such as it was listened by projectors before the trick.

    { "userId": "163082d2-b005-75d7-5eb6-aa1f83efd55d", "eventType": "Debit", "payload": { "amount": 30 }, "version": 3
}

    And this is the shape of the event triggering the projectors that contain the aggregate.

    { "aggregate": { "lastKnownVersion": 3, "totalBalance": 500, "lastMonthBalance": -20 }, "lastEvent": { "userId": "163082d2-b005-75d7-5eb6-aa1f83efd55d", "eventType": "Debit", "payload": { "amount": 30 }, "version": 3 }
}

    ✅ The job of the projectors become trivial

    The aggregate represents the current state of the data at the version of the latest event taken into account. Furthermore, there is no rule about the shape of the aggregate and it can be customized in the most exploitable way. For this use case, it can be built with keys being all the projections.

    { "lastKnownVersion": 3, "totalBalance": 500, "lastMonthBalance": -20
}

    With an access to the aggregate, projectors have at their disposal the exact value of the projection they each handle and the last version of this data. So it's not necessary to update the projection based on the previous value anymore. The projector can simply use the PutItem command of DynamoDB to overwrite the previous value only if the version incoming is strictly superior to current one stored. The projections become idempotent and they allow more flexibility in the way they are triggered.

    🔂 The replay is then extremely simple 👌

    It's as simple as writing a replay type event in the ledger. All projectors need to listen to the corresponding EventBridge event. Then every time a replay type event is written in the ledger, all projectors update their projection with their latest version.

    🔖 Do not forget to ignore this replay type event in the reducer when computing the aggregate

    📣 A possible drawback of the dispatchAggregate lambda

    The computation time of the dispatchAggregate lambda adds run time to the process. But it is acceptable after checking the results we have on our lambda.

    The run time of the dispatchAggregate lambda with a cold start is 250ms on average and pretty much consistently.

    But the run time of the same lambda when it's already hot is between 30ms and 80ms for the slowest runs, depending on the userId in question.

    The latency is very low in general because there is only one gateway through which the events go, therefore the lambda is hot most of the time.

    The run time topic may become an issue when the number of events becomes enormous.

    For computing the aggregate, all events must be fetched and reduced within the lambda and it might be really long to handle that much events. But here again, there is a solution. In fact, snapshots of the aggregate can be stored in the event ledger. A snapshot representing the aggregate of version N allow not to worry about events of versions strictly inferior to N and start from this one when computing the aggregate.

    📈 What's left for us to do to implement the most effective solution with the latest releases

    Both the issues I've mentioned about the retry policy of the lambda plugged to the streams and the number of lambda you can plug to a stream is no longer true since this summer 2022.

    In terms of efficiency, we still need a single lambda to compute the aggregate since it's time-consuming operation, so the rule of plugging multiple lambda to the streams is not a rule that will change this architecture.

    The opportunity here is that we can have the lambda dispatching the events to the projectors failing without worrying about and infinite retry or loosing data. The error handling allows the configuration of destination for failed-events and for a retry policy of my choice. Having both a fanout that must not fail and a dispatchAggregate to compute and attach the aggregate to the event becomes useless. A step can now be skipped by having the dispatchAggregate receiving the DynamoDB streams, computing and attaching the aggregate to the events before publishing them in EventBridge. A retry policy and a destination for failed-events must be configured on this lambda.

    This should make it the most efficient and up to date solution for synchronizing data between an event ledger and projections in an event sourcing application.

    🎁 We have learned a lot about event-sourcing at Kumo in many projects. We centralized all these learnings in a library: Castore
item.author: maximevivier

item.title: REST API 与 ASP.NET 6 y MySQL | REST API con ASP.NET 6 y MySQL
item.description:
丹尼尔·戈麦斯·贾拉米洛

发表于你好！ 必要的递归：

视觉工作室 2019/2022。

  • 处理一个 seguir：

    注意： para encontrar el centro de administración de los paquetes NuGet podemos dirigirnos al menu de opciones -> proyecto -> manejar paquetes NuGet。

    突击队：

     Scaffold-DbContext "server=servername;port=portnumber;user=username;password=pass;database=databasename" MySql.EntityFrameworkCore -OutputDir Entities -f

    El resultado es el siguiente: El resultado es el siguiente:

    Aquí, la clase User está definida de la siguiente manera: Aquí, la clase User está definida de la siguiente manera:

     public partial class User { public int Id { get ; set ; } public string FirstName { get ; set ; } public string LastName { get ; set ; } public string Username { get ; set ; } public string Password { get ; set ; } public DateTime EnrollmentDate { get ; set ; } }

    Y el DBContext , el cual tiene la configuración con la base de datos, cuyo método principal OnConfiguring se verá algo como esto: Y el DBContext , el cual tiene la configuración con la base de datos, cuyo método principal OnConfiguring se verá algo como esto:

     protected override void OnConfiguring ( DbContextOptionsBuilder optionsBuilder ) { if (! optionsBuilder . IsConfigured ) { optionsBuilder . UseMySQL ( "server=localhost;port=3306;user=root;password=;database=database" ); } }

    Ahora, no es lo más adecuado que la cadena de conexión a la base de datos se encuentre especificada en este método OnConfiguring . Ahora, no es lo más adecuado que la cadena de conxión a la base de datos se encuentre especificada en este método OnConfiguring 。 Para esto, dentro de nuestro proyecto podemos encontrar el archivo appsettings.json , en el cual podremos definir esta configuración: Para esto，dentro de nuestro proyecto podemos encontrar el archivo appsettings.json ，en el cual podremos definir esta configuración：

     "AllowedHosts" : "*" ,
"ConnectionStrings" : { "DefaultConnection" : "server=servername;port=portnumber;user=username;password=pass;database=databasename;"
}

    清醒的地毯Controllers ，agregaremos un controlador llamado UserController ：

    接下来是什么？

    为阅读者致谢：

    在Twitter / esDanielGomez.com上没有任何问题！

    一旦暂停，esdanielgomez 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    • 一旦解除暂停，esdanielgomez 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，esdanielgomez 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 esdanielgomez 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 esdanielgomez 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 esdanielgomez 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Daniel Gomez Jaramillo

    Posted on Oct 6

    ¡Hola! En este artículo tutorial aprenderemos a construir una web API desde ASP.NET 6 para manejar operaciones CRUD con una base de datos en MySQL.

    Nota: el código fuente empleado en este tutorial está disponible en GitHub en el siguiente repositorio: ASP.NET 6 Web API.

    Recursos necesarios:

    Para seguir paso a paso este articulo o ejecutar el demo incluido, es necesario tener en funcionamiento las siguientes herramientas:

    • MySQL.
    • .NET 6 SDK.
    • Visual Studio 2019/2022.
    • La carga de trabajo desarrollo web y ASP.NET para Visual Studio 2019/2022.

    Proceso a seguir:

    En el tutorial tendremos tres partes importantes:

    1. Revisar la base de datos que vamos a utilizar.
    2. Establecer el acceso a la base de datos desde ASP.NET a través de Entity Framework.
    3. Establecer los controladores y sus métodos para el servicio web.

    Como caso de estudio para este tutorial se manejarán los datos de usuarios a través de operaciones CRUD (Crear, Leer, Actualizar, y Eliminar) en registros.

    1. La base de datos para el domino de la aplicación.

    La base de datos que utilizaremos en este ejemplo está conformada por una única tabla llamada: User, con los atributos: Id, FirstName, LastName, Username, Password, y EnrrollmentDate; en MySQL.

    La sentencia SQL para la creación de la tabla User es la siguiente:

    CREATE TABLE `user` ( `Id` INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, `FirstName` VARCHAR(45) NOT NULL, `LastName` VARCHAR(45) NOT NULL, `Username` VARCHAR(45) NOT NULL, `Password` VARCHAR(45) NOT NULL, `EnrollmentDate` datetime NOT NULL );

    Muy bien, con la base de datos establecida, ya podemos comenzar con la implementación de nuestro primer proyecto para el desarrollo de servicios API Rest.

    2. Establecer el acceso a la base de datos desde ASP.NET a través de Entity Framework.

    Proyecto ASP.NET 6 del tipo Web API.

    En Visual Studio lo primero que haremos es crear un nuevo proyecto de tipo ASP.NET Core Web API:

    Después, en los siguientes pasos podremos especificar el Framework.

    Con este proyecto crearemos el acceso a la base de datos e implementaremos un controlador correspondiente para trabajar con esos datos y proporcionar la web API.

    Acceso a la base de datos con Entity Framework.

    Para establecer las entidades a través de clases y la conexión de la base de datos se puede emplear el enfoque Database First de Entity Framework, el cual permite hacer _scaffolding _desde la base de datos hacia el proyecto, es decir, generar clases automáticamente de acuerdo con las entidades establecidas en la base de datos y la conexión en el proyecto.

    Para este propósito, es necesario instalar tres paquetes NuGet:

    • Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design
    • Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools
    • MySql.EntityFrameworkCore

    En caso de que se este trabajando con SQL Server, el paquete NuGet a instalar será: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SQLServer.

    Nota: para encontrar el centro de administración de los paquetes NuGet podemos dirigirnos al menú de opciones -> proyecto -> manejar paquetes NuGet.

    Con la instalación de estos paquetes NuGet, ahora abriremos la consola de administración de paquetes para introducir un comando que permitirá realizar _scaffolding _desde la base de datos:

    Comando:

    Scaffold-DbContext "server=servername;port=portnumber;user=username;password=pass;database=databasename" MySql.EntityFrameworkCore -OutputDir Entities -f

    El resultado es el siguiente:

    Aquí, la clase User está definida de la siguiente manera:

    public partial class User
{ public int Id { get; set; } public string FirstName { get; set; } public string LastName { get; set; } public string Username { get; set; } public string Password { get; set; } public DateTime EnrollmentDate { get; set; }
}

    Y el DBContext, el cual tiene la configuración con la base de datos, cuyo método principal OnConfiguring se verá algo como esto:

    protected override void OnConfiguring(DbContextOptionsBuilder optionsBuilder)
{ if (!optionsBuilder.IsConfigured) { optionsBuilder.UseMySQL("server=localhost;port=3306;user=root;password=;database=database"); }
}

    Ahora, no es lo más adecuado que la cadena de conexión a la base de datos se encuentre especificada en este método OnConfiguring. Para esto, dentro de nuestro proyecto podemos encontrar el archivo appsettings.json, en el cual podremos definir esta configuración:

    "AllowedHosts": "*",
"ConnectionStrings": { "DefaultConnection": "server=servername;port=portnumber;user=username;password=pass;database=databasename;"
}

    Luego, en la clase Program agregaremos como servicio al DBContext, y luego debemos hacer referencia a la propiedad DefaultConnection especificada en el archivo appsettings.json:

    builder.Services.AddEntityFrameworkMySQL() .AddDbContext<DBContext>(options => { options.UseMySQL(builder.Configuration.GetConnectionString("DefaultConnection")); }); });

    En este caso, regresando a la clase del DBContext, borramos la cadena de conexión especificada en el método OnConfiguring. A la final tendríamos el método vacío:

    protected override void OnConfiguring(DbContextOptionsBuilder optionsBuilder)
{}

    Con estos pasos ya tenemos lista la conexión y las configuraciones necesarias para trabajar con la base de datos en ASP.NET con la ayuda de Entity Framework.

    3. Establecer los controladores y sus métodos para el servicio web.

    Con el objetivo de transportar los datos entre los procesos para el manejo de la base de datos y los procesos para trabajar con los servicios web, es recomendable establecer clases DTO por cada entidad del proyecto, en este caso, un DTO para la entidad User.

    Para ello crearemos una nueva carpeta dentro del proyecto llamada DTO y crearemos una clase llamada UserDTO, cuyos atributos serán los mismos que la clase User definida en la sección Entities anteriormente:

    public class UserDTO
{ public int Id { get; set; } public string FirstName { get; set; } public string LastName { get; set; } public string Username { get; set; } public string Password { get; set; } public DateTime EnrollmentDate { get; set; }
}

    Controladores para el Web API.

    Ahora lo que haremos es agregar los controladores, en este caso el controlador para el usuario, el cual permitirá establecer métodos para realizar operaciones CRUD sobre las tablas de la base de datos y exponerlos a través del Web API. Sobre la carpeta Controllers, agregaremos un controlador llamado UserController:

    La definición de la clase y su constructor se verá así:

    [ApiController]
[Route("api/[controller]")]
public class UserController : ControllerBase
{ private readonly DBContext DBContext; public UserController( DBContext DBContext) { this.DBContext = DBContext; } ...
}

    Ahora el objetivo es realizar las operaciones CRUD. En este sentido, utilizaremos métodos para acceder a la información (Get), para insertar datos (Post), para modificar (Put) y para eliminar un registro (Delete).

    A continuación, se muestra el código final de cada uno de los métodos:

    A. Obtener el listado de todos los usuarios registrados.

    [HttpGet("GetUsers")]
public async Task<ActionResult<List<UserDTO>>> Get()
{ var List = await DBContext.User.Select( s => new UserDTO { Id = s.Id, FirstName = s.FirstName, LastName = s.LastName, Username = s.Username, Password = s.Password, EnrollmentDate = s.EnrollmentDate } ).ToListAsync(); if (List.Count < 0) { return NotFound(); } else { return List; }
}

    B. Obtener los datos de un usuario especifico según su Id.

    [HttpGet("GetUserById")]
public async Task<ActionResult<UserDTO>> GetUserById(int Id)
{ UserDTO User = await DBContext.User.Select( s => new UserDTO { Id = s.Id, FirstName = s.FirstName, LastName = s.LastName, Username = s.Username, Password = s.Password, EnrollmentDate = s.EnrollmentDate }) .FirstOrDefaultAsync(s => s.Id == Id); if (User == null) { return NotFound(); } else { return User; }
}

    C. Insertar un nuevo usuario.

    [HttpPost("InsertUser")]
public async Task<HttpStatusCode> InsertUser(UserDTO User)
{ var entity = new User() { FirstName = User.FirstName, LastName = User.LastName, Username = User.Username, Password = User.Password, EnrollmentDate = User.EnrollmentDate }; DBContext.User.Add(entity); await DBContext.SaveChangesAsync(); return HttpStatusCode.Created;
}

    D. Actualizar los datos de un usuario especifico.

    [HttpPut ("UpdateUser")]
public async Task<HttpStatusCode> UpdateUser(UserDTO User)
{ var entity = await DBContext.User.FirstOrDefaultAsync(s => s.Id == User.Id); entity.FirstName = User.FirstName; entity.LastName = User.LastName; entity.Username = User.Username; entity.Password = User.Password; entity.EnrollmentDate = User.EnrollmentDate; await DBContext.SaveChangesAsync(); return HttpStatusCode.OK;
}

    E. Eliminar a un usuario según su Id.

    [HttpDelete("DeleteUser/{Id}")]
public async Task<HttpStatusCode> DeleteUser(int Id)
{ var entity = new User() { Id = Id }; DBContext.User.Attach(entity); DBContext.User.Remove(entity); await DBContext.SaveChangesAsync(); return HttpStatusCode.OK;
}

    Con estos métodos y con los pasos seguidos hasta este punto, el servicio web está listo para ponerse en ejecución.

    Probar la web API implementada

    Para probar la API implementada podemos utilizar Swagger UI, una herramienta visual que nos permite interactuar con los métodos de nuestro servicio, y que a su vez ya se encuentra integrada en nuestro proyecto de ASP.NET 6.

    Para las pruebas, necesitamos compilar y ejecutar la aplicación:

    A continuación, podemos ver la interfaz de Swagger para que podamos realizar las pruebas correspondientes de acuerdo con los métodos definidos en nuestro controlador y de una manera interactiva:

    Al ser este un servicio RestFul, nosotros podemos utilizar cualquier otro programa o aplicación para consumir estos servicios. Por ejemplo, aquí podemos ver una llamada al método GetUsers desde la herramienta Postman:

    What next?

    Con este tutorial hemos aprendido paso a paso como implementar servicios HTTP que manejen datos de usuarios desde ASP.NET 6, y como realizar pruebas con estas funcionalidades.

    El código fuente de este ejemplo puede visualizarse desde el siguiente repositorio en GitHub: ASP.NET 6 Web API.

    Gracias por leer:

    Espero que te haya gustado el artículo. Si tienes alguna pregunta o alguna idea en mente, será un gusto poder estar en comunicación contigo y juntos intercambiar conocimientos entre sí.

    Nos vemos en Twitter / esDanielGomez.com!

    ¡Saludos!

    Ortiz de Arcanjo Antonio David - Sep 5

item.title: 在 VSCode 上进行最低限度的设置以处理 TS/JS 项目 | Bare minimum setup on VSCode to work on TS/JS projects
item.description:
布雷诺阿尔维斯

发表于市场上有很多 VSCode 扩展，但在使用 TS/JS 项目时，哪一个是必要的？

我将介绍在完善的 TS/JS 项目中工作的最低限度的扩展以及一些我认为对我的日常任务非常有帮助的附加功能。

埃斯林特

在需要保持代码一致的环境中与其他开发人员合作时，我们需要有一些东西来确保每个人都遵循某些规则，而 Eslint 正是这样做的。

要使用此扩展，您需要在项目中安装 Eslint 作为开发依赖项和配置文件。

你可以在 Eslint文档中找到一些很好的配置文件示例

这两个工具与 Eslint 一起工作，以帮助开发人员格式化并遵循所有已建立的模式。

要使用 Prettier，您需要将其安装为开发人员依赖项并创建一个更漂亮的配置文件 ( docs )

对于 EditorConfig，您还需要创建一个 editorConfig 文件（文档）

附加功能

包 Json 升级

当您需要轻松可视化可以升级哪些软件包以及哪些版本可用时，此扩展非常有用

开发容器

开发容器在使用 docker 容器时非常有用。

我无法用几句话说这对现在有多大帮助......如果你不知道副驾驶是什么，请查看 youtube 上的一些视频。

一旦暂停，brenoalves 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，brenoalves 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，所有 brenoalve 的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 brenoalves 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 brenoalve 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记的 brenoalve 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Breno Alves

Posted on Oct 6

There are plenty of VSCode Extensions on the marketplace but which one is necessary when it comes to working with TS/JS projects?

I'll cover the bare minimum extensions to work on a well-established TS/JS project and some extras which I find very helpful in my day-to-day tasks.

Eslint

When it comes to working with others developers in environments where we need to keep consistent code, we need to have something to make sure everyone is following certain rules and Eslint does exactly this.

To use this extension you need to install Eslint on your project as a development dependency and a configuration file.

you can find some good examples of configuration files at Eslint docs

Prettier and EditorConfig

Those two tools work alongside Eslint to help developers format and follow all patterns established.

To use Prettier you need to install it as a developer dependency and create a prettier config file (docs)

and for EditorConfig you will need to create an editorConfig file as well (docs)

Extras

Package Json Upgrade

This extension is very helpful when you need to easily visualize which package you can upgrade and which versions are available

Dev Containers

Dev containers is very helpful when working with docker containers.

Github Copilot

I can't put in a few words how much this is helpful nowadays... if you are not aware of what copilot is please check out some videos on youtube about it.

item.title: 轻松实现本地化：在 Flutter 中单击即可生成 100 多种语言 | Localization Made Easy: Generate 100+ Languages with Single Click in Flutter
item.description:
亚什·马坎

发表于嘿开发人员👋 我最近在 Flutter 中制作了一个包，它使 Flutter 中的本地化变得非常容易。 它可以通过一个命令生成 100 多种语言的本地化。 它基本上从项目中提取字符串并使用谷歌翻译来实现这一点。 如果有兴趣，您可以在 pub dev 上查看该软件包，或者我也制作了详细的视频。

包： https ://pub.dev/packages/very_easy_localization
如果您有任何疑问，建议请告诉我，如果您可以为 repo 加注星标并订阅我的频道，那会让我很开心。 希望在另一边见到你...😁

Yash Makan

Hey developers 👋 I have recently made a package in flutter which makes localization in flutter very easy. It can generate localization in 100+ languages with just a single command. It basically extracts strings from the project and uses google translate to achieve this. If interested you can check out the package on pub dev or I have made a detailed video as well.

item.title: 谁有建立网站的想法#hacktoberfest | Who has an website idea to build #hacktoberfest
item.description:
如果你喜欢用 python 为 web 构建东西，请查看我在过去几年中慢慢构建的一个项目。 今年3月开源。

马尔卡塔

这意味着您可以开箱即用地构建一个工作站点，并且可以通过配置使其成为您自己的站点。 您还可以通过编写插件来完成您需要做的事情，从而完全根据您的需求进行定制。

为项目库做出贡献

一旦你建立了一些东西，并在野外托管，把它提交到project-gallery 。 博客启动器

想看一个例子看看启动器

在整个月中，我一直在将我的一些网站转换为官方博客启动器，它使用开箱即用的博客的所有最佳功能。 它包括各种页面，这些页面是有关如何配置各种插件的一般说明。 这些是如何使用它的很好的例子，尤其是在文档正在进行中的时候。

不想举个例子？

如果您没有任何想要在公共场合托管的东西，我仍然会感谢任何人尝试使用它制作项目并留下反馈。 如果您遇到任何问题，请向项目或入门模板提交一个小公关。 如果您有更大的问题，请在投入大量工作之前提出问题并进行讨论。

网站创意

请为自己做一些你想要的东西。 属于你的东西，给你带来快乐。 如果您正在为想法而苦苦挣扎，这里有一些。

你怎么看？

我很想听听你在下面的想法。 查看启动器并告诉我您的想法。



原文:

If you are someone who likes building things with python for the web, check out a project I have been slowly building up over the past few years. Open sourced in March this year.

Markata

Markata is a plugins all the way down static site generator for python. Meaning that you can build a working site from it out of the box, and you can make it your own through configuration. You can also really make it completely customized to your needs by writing plugins to do what you need it to do.

Contribute to the project-gallery

Once you have something built, and hosted in the wild, submit it to the project-gallery. There are instructions inside issue#78.

The Blog Starter

want to see an example check out the starter

Throughout this month I have been converting some of my sites over to an official blog starter that uses all of the best features for a blog out of the box. It includes a variety of pages that are general instructions about how to configure the various plugins. These serve as great examples of how to use it, especially while the docs are a bit of a work in progress.

Don't want to host an example?

If you don't have anything that you want to host in public I would still appreciate anyone to try to make a project with it and leave feedback. If you run into any issues submit a small pr to either the project or the starter template. If you have a larger issue, please raise an issue and discuss it before putting tons of work into it.

Site Ideas

Markata is setup to build about anything you can imagine on the web that can be statically compiled and hosted. Please make something that you want for yourself. Something that is all yours, and brings you joy. If you are struggling with ideas here are a few.

What do you think?

I'd love to hear your thoughts down below. Check out the starter and tell me what you think.
item.author: waylonwalker

item.title: 很棒的开源贡献工具 | Awesome tools for Open Source Contribution
item.description:
我们在 10 月，这是众所周知的开源社区的 Hacktoberfest 月。 在这个月里，初学者开发者学习为开源项目做贡献。

在过去的三年里，我一直在参加这个活动。 去年，我也以维护者的身份参与其中。 今年，我也以维护者的身份参与其中，尽我所能帮助开发者。

因此，我在这里通过提供一些您可以在为开源项目做出贡献时使用的工具来帮助您。 该工具将有助于：

  • 从大型代码库中搜索代码
  • 寻找要解决的问题
  • 用于快速编辑的在线编辑器

那么，让我们开始吧。

纪录片

您的代码库的搜索引擎； 向 Documatic 提出问题并在几秒钟内找到相关代码和见解

问题可以是“如何连接到数据库？” 它会给你关于数据库的代码。 该代码不需要任何与数据库相关的词，但它会返回与此相关的所有代码。 最相关的代码片段将位于顶部。

您想要贡献的存储库，在您的 GitHub 个人资料中分叉它。 分叉后，将您的 GitHub 个人资料与 Documatic 连接并开始搜索代码库。 这将节省您的时间，因为您不必手动搜索或等待维护者的评论。

您可以在以下位置查看平台： https ://app.documatic.com/

您可以在这里尝试平台的工作： https ://www.askyourcode.com/

代码分类

发现开始为开源做出贡献的最简单方法。 超过 66,078 名开发人员正在使用我们免费的社区开发工具帮助 7,320 个项目

您可以按 100 多种编程语言过滤存储库。 存储库是红色、黄色和绿色，分别显示项目的复杂性为高级、中级和初学者友好。

好第一期

做出你的第一个开源贡献比你想象的要容易。 从今天开始！

您可以探索 20 多种编程语言。 您可以找到每个存储库的描述、语言、星标和上次活动。

启动全新的自动化开发环境
以秒为单位，在云端完成每个任务。

代码编辑器是 VS Code 的在线版本。 我用它作为 chrome 扩展。 每当我需要在合并之前进行快速编辑或验证代码时，我都会使用 GitPod。

你可以从这里下载它作为 chrome 扩展

自述文件

创建自述文件的最简单方法

拖放功能使您能够为标题和贡献指南选择模板、在本地运行以及自述文件的其他重要方面。 您可以轻松地将创建的文件下载到名为README.md的 markdown(.md) 中。

与我联系

我希望，这套工具可以帮助你在开源之旅中。 参加 Hacktoberfest 和其他黑客马拉松，充分利用这个月。

感谢您阅读博文。



原文:

We are in October which is well known as the month of Hacktoberfest in the open-source community. During this month, beginner developers learn to contribute to open-source projects.

I am participating in this event for the last three years. Last year, I also participated as a maintainer. This year, I am also participating as a maintainer and trying my best to help the developers.

So, I am here to help you by providing some tools you can use while contributing to an open-source project. This tool will be helpful in:

  • Searching Code from large codebases
  • Finding Issues to resolve
  • Online Editor for Quick edit

So, let's get started.

Documatic

A search engine for your codebase; Ask Documatic a question and find relevant code and insights in seconds

Documatic is a platform where you can find relevant code from a codebase by just asking a question. The question can be "How to connect to the database?" and it will give you code regarding the database. The code need not have any word related to the database but it will return you the all code related to this. The most relevant code snippets will be at the top.

The repository to which you want to contribute, fork it in your GitHub profile. After forking, connect your GitHub profile with Documatic and start searching the codebase. This will save you time as you don't have to manually search or wait for the maintainer's comment.

You can check the platform at: https://app.documatic.com/

You can try the working of the platform here: https://www.askyourcode.com/

CodeTriage

Discover the easiest way to get started contributing to open source. Over 66,078 devs are helping 7,320 projects with our free, community-developed tools

CodeTriage is an awesome platform to find open-source repositories to contribute. You can filter the repository by more than 100 programming languages. The repositories are red, yellow, and green showing the complexity of the project as Advanced, Intermediate, and Beginner-friendly respectively.

Good First Issue

Making your first open-source contribution is easier than you think. Good First Issue is a curated list of issues from popular open-source projects that you can fix easily. Start today!

Good First Issue provides you with the issue that is more likely to be solved by beginner developers. You can explore more than 20 programming languages. You can find the description, language, stars, and last activity of every repository.

GitPod

Spin up fresh, automated dev environments
for each task, in the cloud, in seconds.

GitPod provides you with an online environment in a workspace to code. The code editor is the online version of VS Code. I used it as a chrome extension. Whenever I need to do a quick edit or to verify the code before merging I use GitPod.

You can download it as the chrome extension from here

readme.so

The easiest way to create a README

Readme.so is one of the best platforms to generate readme for your project. The drag-and-drop features enable you to choose templates for Title, and Contribution guidelines, run locally, and other important aspects of a Readme file. You can easily download the created file in a markdown(.md) with the name README.md.

Connect with Me

I hope, this set of tools will help you in the open-source journey. Take full advantage of this month by participating in Hacktoberfest and other hackathons.

Thanks for reading the blog post.
item.author: surajondev

item.title: HacktoberFest：第 1 周 | HacktoberFest: Week 1
item.description:
我以前从未从事过开源项目。 最接近的一个是必须在我同学的存储库上工作以添加功能或解决问题。

问题

我注意到他的“复选框”没有按照自述文件中的预期执行。

使固定

我只是简单地编辑了文件以使其看起来更美观，并且还使复选框按预期工作

  • 第二期
  • 拉取请求

问题

我注意到，在他的网站上创建一个新帐户时，没有对密码的“强”程度进行适当的验证。 目前这可能不是问题，因为它们目前处于早期阶段，但最好是当前创建的帐户遵循与将来创建的帐户相同的格式。

创建帐户

报名

如上图所示，“一个”字符足以让用户创建帐户。 对于该电子邮件被黑客入侵的容易程度，这并不是一个好主意。

设置

除了在弄清楚 REACT 是如何工作的，以及他们如何编写组件方面刷新我的想法之外，没有什么可以为作业做准备的。

我一直很喜欢 REACT，但自从 7 月以来我就没有碰过它，所以我目前不习惯它是如何工作的。

编码

修复相当简单，创建一个新函数，并在输入时调用它而不是标准函数。

更改 onInput 值

值从{[handleInput]}更改为名为{[handlePassword]}的新函数

创建一个新函数来处理密码验证

该函数有两个 if 语句来处理缺少“大写”和“数字”

添加了一个新的 customValidity ，无论他们是否每次都更改密码

演示

该程序现在提示用户提供至少 12 个字符的最小长度，并且首先是大写

接下来，他们将验证用户是否缺少数值

完成所有验证后，用户就可以毫无问题地注册。

研究

  • 我搜索了如何在 js 上处理正则表达式
    • 我需要这个来确定提供的字符串是否包含任何大写或数值
    • 这是为了缩短代码，而不是使用 for 循环来检查每个字符串索引

困难

最困难的部分是必须弄清楚如何检查字符串是否包含大写字母或数字。 研究它导致人们告诉我使用 for 循环，但我发现它效率低下。 经过一段时间的搜索，我遇到了.test从长远来看它帮助了我的代码



原文:

I've never worked on an open source projects before. The closest one is having to work on my classmate's repository to add features, or fix problems.

Problem

I noticed that his "checkbox" aren't doing as intended in the README file.

Fix

I simply edited the file to look more presentable, and also made the checkbox work as intended

  • Second Issue
  • Pull Request

Problem

I noticed that when creating a new account at his website, that the there are no proper validation in terms of how "strong" a password is. This might not be an issue currently for that they are currently at an early stage, but it's best that the accounts created currently follows the same format as those that will be created in the future.

Creating Account

Signing Up

As seen in the images above, "one" character was enough to allow the user to create an account. That's not really a good idea for how easy it is for that email to get hacked.

Setup

There's nothing much to learn to prepare for the assignment, other than refreshing my mind in terms of figuring out how REACT works, and how they write their components.

I have always liked REACT, but I haven't touched it ever since July so I'm currently not used to how it works.

Coding

The fix was fairly simple, creating a new function, and calling it instead of the standard function when on input.

Changing the onInput value

the value is changed from {[handleInput]} to a new function called {[handlePassword]}

Creating a new function to handle password validation

The function has two if statements to handle the lack of "uppercase" and "number"

A new customValidity is added for which if statement it goes through, regardless if they change the password each time

Demo

The program now prompts the user to provide a minimum length of 12 characters, and an uppercase first

Next they will validate the user if they are lacking a numerical value

Once all the validation are done, the user is able to signup without any problems.

Research

  • I searched how to handle Regular Expression on js
    • I needed this to figure out whether the string provided contains any uppercase or numerical value
    • This is to shorten the code, and not use a for loop to check for each string index

Difficulties

The most difficult part is having to figure out how to check if a string contains an uppercase or numerical. Researching about it leads to people telling me to use a for loop, but I found it inefficient. After a while of searching I came across .test which helped my code in a long run
item.author: ririio

item.title: javascrip代码没有运行 | javascrip code not running
item.description:
约布拉王

yobra-king

Posted on Oct 6

a = [3,26,46,74,86,83,84,84,74,74,74]; for(r = 0; r< 11; r = r + r){ sum = 0; sum = sum + a[r];

};

Mahmoud Harmouch - Sep 24

Suraj Vishwakarma - Oct 3

Once suspended, yobraking will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, yobraking will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging yobraking will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 为什么我使用 JMH | Why I'm using JMH
item.description:
博切宁

发表于我的任务是调查在客户环境中类型化枚举每个实例占用 13 Mb 的原因。 类型化枚举是一个域对象，允许用户为自定义字段配置枚举类型。
一旦枚举有大约 1k 个元素，用例就可以重现。

所以我可以假设以下可能的原因：

  • 内存泄漏
    • 在内存中保留一些过时的缓存值
  • 在内存中存储大文件
    • 枚举由用户上传为XML文件，因此可以缓存一些中间大对象
  • 复制数据（如果是，那为什么？）。
    • 在缓存中为不同的键存储相同数据的多个副本

我做了什么来找到原因

首先，我在本地重新运行了该场景，进行了堆转储，并使用MemoryAnalizer对其进行了分析。
从下面的报告中可以看出（直方图 -> 在过滤器中选择 TypedEnumeration -> 具有传出引用的对象列表 -> 按保留堆排序），我们正在为control2Options字段中的每个键存储重复数据。

 Class Name | Shallow Heap | Retained Heap ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- TypedEnumeration @ 0xdedf8050 | 64 | 4,637,960 |- control2Options java.util.HashMap @ 0xdef01f68 | 48 | 3,925,856 | |- table java.util.HashMap$Node[32] @ 0xdf7d5b10 | 144 | 3,925,808 | | |- [26] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf7d5ba0 | 32 | 712,256 | | |- [4] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfc3b790 | 32 | 712,256 | | |- [14] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf12a800 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [13] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf24eab8 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [22] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf3a9608 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [23] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfa2e9a0 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [11] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfa50ff0 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [6] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfb91928 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [5] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfd3ca10 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [0] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xde742630 | 32 | 4,128 | | |- [19] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf38cad0 | 32 | 4,128 | | |- class java.util.HashMap$Node[] @ 0xd444d5a8 | 0 | 0 | | '- Total: 12 entries | | | |- <class> class java.util.HashMap @ 0xd4450dd0 System Class | 40 | 144 | '- Total: 2 entries | | |- allOptions java.util.ArrayList @ 0xdedf8090 | 24 | 8,040 ..... '- Total: 10 entries | | TypedEnumeration @ 0xe0064a60 | 64 | 4,637,960 |- control2Options java.util.HashMap @ 0xe055ecb0 | 48 | 3,925,856 | |- table java.util.HashMap$Node[32] @ 0xe08dd030 | 144 | 3,925,808 | | |- [4] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe086f630 | 32 | 712,256 | | |- [26] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe08dd0c0 | 32 | 712,256 | | |- [23] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe053daf8 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [5] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe084cd10 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [13] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe08521f0 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [6] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe08dc138 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [11] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe09de780 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [14] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe0a665c0 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [22] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe0acc8c0 | 32 | 356,128 | | |- [0] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfa1e7d0 | 32 | 4,128 | | |- [19] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe0aefcb0 | 32 | 4,128 | | |- class java.util.HashMap$Node[] @ 0xd444d5a8 | 0 | 0 | | '- Total: 12 entries | | | |- <class> class java.util.HashMap @ 0xd4450dd0 System Class | 40 | 144 | '- Total: 2 entries | | |- allOptions java.util.ArrayList @ 0xe0064b08 | 24 | 8,040 .... '- Total: 10 entries | |

让我们在启用探查器的情况下重新运行场景。
我使用过JMC和Flight Records ，它告诉我应用程序在引导期间占用了额外的内存。

不完全是那个地方，但它显着缩小了范围，经过一些代码调查，我发现应用程序在哪里浪费内存。

成功？ 不，我仍然需要解决问题并证明我们的解决方案正在解决问题。

这就是JMH上台的地方。

我们可以用这个工具做什么

它关心内存对齐、方法内联、预热以及所有其他使基准测试难以编写的东西。 它还有多个分析器，您可以在基准测试中使用。 

 - Profiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) |- ExternalProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | |- AbstractPerfAsmProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | | |- DTraceAsmProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | | |- LinuxPerfAsmProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | | '- WinPerfAsmProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | |- AsyncProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | |- JavaFlightRecorderProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | |- LinuxPerfC2CProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | |- LinuxPerfNormProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | |- LinuxPerfProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) | '- SafepointsProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) '- InternalProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) |- AsyncProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) |- ClassloaderProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) |- CompilerProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) |- GCProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) |- JavaFlightRecorderProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) |- PausesProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile) '- StackProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)

我用过的一个是GCProfiler 。 分析器在基准执行期间计算内存分配和垃圾收集。

我感兴趣的统计数据是gc.alloc.rate (MB/sec)和gc.alloc.rate.norm (B/op)


考虑到调查结果，我可能会假设应用程序分配的内存更少，GC 压力更小，应用程序存储在缓存中的数据也更少。

警告：该假设仅对上述问题是公平的，因为调查显示应用程序正在复制它可能永远不会使用的数据。

所以我最终制定了以下计划：

  • 为可疑方法编写基准
  • 使用 GCProfiler 运行基准测试并获得一些数字
  • 改进代码或修复问题
  • 再次运行基准测试
  • 分析数字
  • 重复直到成功
public class TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark { @Benchmark public Object newObject(TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution execution) { return new BenchmarkTypeEnumeration(...); } @Benchmark public Object getAvailableOptions(TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution execution) { return new BenchmarkTypeEnumeration(...).getAvailableOptions(null); } @Benchmark public Object getAllOptions(TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution execution) { return new BenchmarkTypeEnumeration(...).getAllSortedOptions(); } @Benchmark public void preLoadEnumeration(TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution execution, Blackhole blackhole) { BenchmarkTypeEnumeration enumeration = new BenchmarkTypeEnumeration(...); blackhole.consume(enumeration.getAllSortedOptions()); for (String controlKey : execution.controlKeys) { blackhole.consume(enumeration.getAvailableOptions(controlKey)); } } }

同时，我会针对标称用例运行最简单的性能基准测试，以防止性能急剧下降。

 public class TypeEnumerationBenchmark { @Benchmark public Object getAvailableOptions(Execution execution) { return execution.enumeration.getAvailableOptions(null); } @Benchmark public Object getAllOptions(Execution execution) { return execution.enumeration.getAllSortedOptions(); } public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception { Options opt = new OptionsBuilder() .parent(new CommandLineOptions(args)) .include(".*TypeEnumerationBenchmark.*") .build(); new Runner(opt).run(); } }

它是如何工作的

因此，一旦您运行基准测试，它将尝试在类路径中查找生成的类，并使用它们来运行实际方法并收集结果。

如何运行它

有几种方法可以运行它。

结果更精确的最好方法是编写main方法，构建一个 .jar 文件并运行它。

 public class Benchmark { .... public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception { Options opt = new OptionsBuilder() .jvmArgsAppend("-Djmh.separateClasspathJAR=true") .parent(new CommandLineOptions(args)) .include(".*Benchmark.*") .build(); new Runner(opt).run(); } }

您也可以从 IDE 运行main方法，但您的 IDE 可能会在 JVM 中添加一些代理，因此结果不太精确。

如果您不需要精确的结果而是查看性能趋势，您可以将测试作为 JUnit 测试运行。

 public class Benchmark { .... @Benchmark public void benchmarkMethod(...) { ... } @Test public void shouldRunBenchmark() throws Exception { Options opt = new OptionsBuilder() .include(".*Benchmark.benchmarkMethod*") .build(); new Runner(opt).run(); } }

我选择从 IDE 运行是因为我对内存分配很感兴趣（IDE 代理的一个小副作用）并且我将在错误修复期间使用它（我不想在 IDE 和控制台之间切换）。

一旦你运行它，你会看到类似

# JMH version: 1.35 # VM version: JDK 11.0.15, OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM, 11.0.15+9-LTS # VM invoker: C:\workdir\bin\amazon-correto\jdk11.0.15_9\bin\java.exe # VM options: -ea -Didea.test.cyclic.buffer.size=1048576 -javaagent:C:\Program Files\JetBrains\IntelliJ IDEA 2022.2.1\lib\idea_rt.jar=50905:C:\Program Files\JetBrains\IntelliJ IDEA 2022.2.1\bin -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 -Djmh.separateClasspathJAR=true # Blackhole mode: full + dont-inline hint (auto-detected, use -Djmh.blackhole.autoDetect=false to disable) # Warmup: 5 iterations, 10 s each # Measurement: 5 iterations, 10 s each # Timeout: 10 min per iteration # Threads: 1 thread, will synchronize iterations # Benchmark mode: Throughput, ops/time # Benchmark: benchmark.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration # Parameters: (controlKeysCount = 10, enumsCount = 10, prefixesCount = 1) # Run progress: 0.00% complete, ETA 00:08:20 # Fork: 1 of 5 # Warmup Iteration 1: 100917.316 ops/s # Warmup Iteration 2: 105528.728 ops/s # Warmup Iteration 3: 105569.479 ops/s # Warmup Iteration 4: 104947.364 ops/s # Warmup Iteration 5: 105071.139 ops/s Iteration 1: 104137.948 ops/s ·gc.alloc.rate: 1900.765 MB/sec ·gc.alloc.rate.norm: 20120.000 B/op ·gc.churn.G1_Eden_Space: 1906.794 MB/sec ·gc.churn.G1_Eden_Space.norm: 20183.824 B/op ·gc.churn.G1_Old_Gen: 0.002 MB/sec ·gc.churn.G1_Old_Gen.norm: 0.018 B/op ·gc.count: 33.000 counts ·gc.time: 22.000 ms Iteration 2: 104582.692 ops/s .... # Run complete. Total time: 00:08:22 REMEMBER: The numbers below are just data. To gain reusable insights, you need to follow up on why the numbers are the way they are. Use profilers (see -prof, -lprof), design factorial experiments, perform baseline and negative tests that provide experimental control, make sure the benchmarking environment is safe on JVM/OS/HW level, ask for reviews from the domain experts. Do not assume the numbers tell you what you want them to tell. Benchmark (controlKeysCount) (enumsCount) (prefixesCount) Mode Cnt Score Error Units TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration 10 1000 1 thrpt 10 104137.948 ± 31.242 ops/s TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.alloc.rate 10 1000 1 thrpt 10 1900.765 ± 48.356 MB/sec TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.alloc.rate.norm 10 1000 1 thrpt 10 20120.000 ± 39.771 B/op TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.churn.G1_Eden_Space 10 1000 1 thrpt 10 1906.794 ± 58.341 MB/sec TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.churn.G1_Eden_Space.norm 10 1000 1 thrpt 10 20183.824 ± 40835.901 B/op TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.churn.G1_Old_Gen 10 1000 1 thrpt 10 0.002 ± 0.027 MB/sec TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.churn.G1_Old_Gen.norm 10 1000 1 thrpt 10 0.018 ± 0.012 B/op TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.count 10 1000 1 thrpt 10 33.000 counts TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.time 10 1000 1 thrpt 10 22.000 ms ....

在哪里：

  • 每个方法都执行了几次。 可以使用-f选项更改数字。 默认值为5 。 预热结果不会用作基准测试结果，主要需要消除一些内部 Java 编译和优化
  • 您可以在此处找到更多执行选项

    马克西米利安·阿鲁达 - 一旦暂停，vbochenin 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，vbochenin 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发表，vbochenin 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 vbochenin 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 vbochenin 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 vbochenin 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

vbochenin

Posted on Oct 6

My task was to investigate the reason why typed enumeration occupies 13 Mb per instance in a customer environment. The typed enumeration is a domain object allowing to users configure enumeration type for custom fields.
The use case is reproducible once enumeration has around 1k elements.

So I may assume the following possible causes:

  • a memory leak
    • keeping in memory some stale caches values
  • storing big files in memory
    • the enumeration is uploaded by user as XML file, so some intermediate big object can be cached
  • duplicating of data (if so, then why?).
    • multiple copies of same data sotred in cach for different keys

What I did to find the cause

First, I've rerun the scenario locally, made a heap dump, and analyzed it with MemoryAnalizer.
It is visible from the report below (Histogram -> Choose TypedEnumeration in filter -> List of object with outgoing reference -> Sort by Retained Heap), that we are storing duplicated data for each key in control2Options field.

Class Name                                                              | Shallow Heap | Retained Heap
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TypedEnumeration @ 0xdedf8050                                           |           64 |     4,637,960
|- control2Options java.util.HashMap @ 0xdef01f68                       |           48 |     3,925,856
|  |- table java.util.HashMap$Node[32] @ 0xdf7d5b10                     |          144 |     3,925,808
|  |  |- [26] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf7d5ba0                       |           32 |       712,256
|  |  |- [4] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfc3b790                        |           32 |       712,256
|  |  |- [14] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf12a800                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [13] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf24eab8                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [22] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf3a9608                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [23] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfa2e9a0                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [11] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfa50ff0                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [6] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfb91928                        |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [5] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfd3ca10                        |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [0] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xde742630                        |           32 |         4,128
|  |  |- [19] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdf38cad0                       |           32 |         4,128
|  |  |-  class java.util.HashMap$Node[] @ 0xd444d5a8                   |            0 |             0
|  |  '- Total: 12 entries                                              |              |              
|  |- <class> class java.util.HashMap @ 0xd4450dd0 System Class         |           40 |           144
|  '- Total: 2 entries                                                  |              |              
|- allOptions java.util.ArrayList @ 0xdedf8090                          |           24 |         8,040
.....
'- Total: 10 entries                                                    |              |              
TypedEnumeration @ 0xe0064a60                                           |           64 |     4,637,960
|- control2Options java.util.HashMap @ 0xe055ecb0                       |           48 |     3,925,856
|  |- table java.util.HashMap$Node[32] @ 0xe08dd030                     |          144 |     3,925,808
|  |  |- [4] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe086f630                        |           32 |       712,256
|  |  |- [26] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe08dd0c0                       |           32 |       712,256
|  |  |- [23] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe053daf8                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [5] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe084cd10                        |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [13] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe08521f0                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [6] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe08dc138                        |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [11] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe09de780                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [14] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe0a665c0                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [22] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe0acc8c0                       |           32 |       356,128
|  |  |- [0] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xdfa1e7d0                        |           32 |         4,128
|  |  |- [19] java.util.HashMap$Node @ 0xe0aefcb0                       |           32 |         4,128
|  |  |-  class java.util.HashMap$Node[] @ 0xd444d5a8                   |            0 |             0
|  |  '- Total: 12 entries                                              |              |              
|  |- <class> class java.util.HashMap @ 0xd4450dd0 System Class         |           40 |           144
|  '- Total: 2 entries                                                  |              |              
|- allOptions java.util.ArrayList @ 0xe0064b08                          |           24 |         8,040
....
'- Total: 10 entries                                                    |              |

Lets rerun the scenario with the profiler turned on.
I've used JMC and Flight Records, and it showed me that the application occupies the extra memory during bootstrap.

Not exactly the place, but it reduced the scope significantly and after some code investigation, I found where the application is wasting memory.

Success? Nope, I still need to fix the issue and prove that our fix is fixing something.

This is where JMH is coming to the stage.

What we can do with the tool

JMH is a microbenchmark framework for Java. It cares about memory alignments, method inlines, warmups, and all other stuff that makes benchmarks hard to write. It also has multiple profilers you may use in your benchmarks.

- Profiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |- ExternalProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   |- AbstractPerfAsmProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   |   |- DTraceAsmProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   |   |- LinuxPerfAsmProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   |   '- WinPerfAsmProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   |- AsyncProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   |- JavaFlightRecorderProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   |- LinuxPerfC2CProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   |- LinuxPerfNormProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   |- LinuxPerfProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    |   '- SafepointsProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
    '- InternalProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
        |- AsyncProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
        |- ClassloaderProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
        |- CompilerProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
        |- GCProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
        |- JavaFlightRecorderProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
        |- PausesProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)
        '- StackProfiler (org.openjdk.jmh.profile)

One I've used is GCProfiler. The profiler calculates memory allocations and garbage collections during benchmark execution.

Statistics it shows, I'm interested in, is gc.alloc.rate (MB/sec) and gc.alloc.rate.norm (B/op)


gc.alloc.rate shows how much memory the benchmark occupies during the trial, but gc.alloc.rate.norm is about how much memory was allocated during single benchmark execution.

Having investigation results in mind, I may assume that less memory the application allocated, less GC pressure and less data application is storing in cache.

Warning: the assumption is fair only for the issue above because the investigation showed application is duplicating data it may never use.

So I've ended up with the following plan:

  • write benchmarks for suspicious methods
  • run benchmarks with GCProfiler and get some numbers
  • improve code or fix the issue
  • run the benchmark again
  • analyze numbers
  • repeat until successful 
public class TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark {  

    @Benchmark  
    public Object newObject(TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution execution) {  
        return new BenchmarkTypeEnumeration(...);  
    }  

    @Benchmark  
    public Object getAvailableOptions(TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution execution) {  
        return new BenchmarkTypeEnumeration(...).getAvailableOptions(null);  
    }  

    @Benchmark  
    public Object getAllOptions(TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution execution) {  
        return new BenchmarkTypeEnumeration(...).getAllSortedOptions();  
    }  

    @Benchmark  
    public void preLoadEnumeration(TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution execution, Blackhole blackhole) {  
        BenchmarkTypeEnumeration enumeration = new BenchmarkTypeEnumeration(...);  
        blackhole.consume(enumeration.getAllSortedOptions());  
        for (String controlKey : execution.controlKeys) {  
            blackhole.consume(enumeration.getAvailableOptions(controlKey));  
        }  
    }  
}

In parallel, I would run the simplest performance benchmark for the nominal use cases to prevent dramatic performance degradation.

public class TypeEnumerationBenchmark {  

    @Benchmark  
    public Object getAvailableOptions(Execution execution) {  
        return execution.enumeration.getAvailableOptions(null);  
    }  

    @Benchmark  
    public Object getAllOptions(Execution execution) {  
        return execution.enumeration.getAllSortedOptions();  
    }  

    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {  
        Options opt = new OptionsBuilder()  
                .parent(new CommandLineOptions(args))  
                .include(".*TypeEnumerationBenchmark.*")  
                .build();  

        new Runner(opt).run();  
    }  
}

How does it work

JMH works with an Annotation Processing API and generates multiple classes for each benchmark method (method marked with @Benchmark annotation).

benchmark
    jmh_generated
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution_jmhType
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution_jmhType_B1
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution_jmhType_B2
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypedEnumerationBenchmarkExecution_jmhType_B3
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark_getAllOptions_jmhTest
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark_getAvailableOptions_jmhTest
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark_jmhType
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark_jmhType_B1
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark_jmhType_B2
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark_jmhType_B3
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark_newObject_jmhTest
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark_preLoadEnumeration_jmhTest
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_Execution_jmhType
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_Execution_jmhType_B1
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_Execution_jmhType_B2
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_Execution_jmhType_B3
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_getAllOptions_jmhTest
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_getAvailableOptions_jmhTest
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_jmhType
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_jmhType_B1
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_jmhType_B2
        benchmark.jmh_generated.TypeEnumerationBenchmark_jmhType_B3
    benchmark.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark
    benchmark.TypeEnumerationBenchmark

So, once you run your benchmark, it will try to find the generated classes in a classpath and use them to run the actual method and collect results.

How to run it

There are the few ways how to run it.

The best one with more precise results is to write the main method, build a .jar file, and run it.

public class Benchmark {

    ....

    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {  
        Options opt = new OptionsBuilder()  
                .jvmArgsAppend("-Djmh.separateClasspathJAR=true")  
                .parent(new CommandLineOptions(args))  
                .include(".*Benchmark.*")  
                .build();  

        new Runner(opt).run();
    }
}

You may also run the main method from IDE, but likely your IDE will add some agent into JVM, so it makes the result less precise.

If you don't need to have precise results but rather to see performance trends, you may run the test as a JUnit test.

public class Benchmark {

    ....
    @Benchmark  
    public void benchmarkMethod(...) {
        ...
    }

    @Test  
    public void shouldRunBenchmark() throws Exception {  
        Options opt = new OptionsBuilder()  
                .include(".*Benchmark.benchmarkMethod*")  
                .build();  

        new Runner(opt).run();  
    }
}

I've chosen to run from IDE because I'm interesting in memory allocations (a small side effect from IDE agents) and I'm going to use it during bugfix (I don't want to switch between IDE and console).

Once you run it you will see something like

# JMH version: 1.35
# VM version: JDK 11.0.15, OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM, 11.0.15+9-LTS
# VM invoker: C:\workdir\bin\amazon-correto\jdk11.0.15_9\bin\java.exe
# VM options: -ea -Didea.test.cyclic.buffer.size=1048576 -javaagent:C:\Program Files\JetBrains\IntelliJ IDEA 2022.2.1\lib\idea_rt.jar=50905:C:\Program Files\JetBrains\IntelliJ IDEA 2022.2.1\bin -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 -Djmh.separateClasspathJAR=true
# Blackhole mode: full + dont-inline hint (auto-detected, use -Djmh.blackhole.autoDetect=false to disable)
# Warmup: 5 iterations, 10 s each
# Measurement: 5 iterations, 10 s each
# Timeout: 10 min per iteration
# Threads: 1 thread, will synchronize iterations
# Benchmark mode: Throughput, ops/time
# Benchmark: benchmark.TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration
# Parameters: (controlKeysCount = 10, enumsCount = 10, prefixesCount = 1)

# Run progress: 0.00% complete, ETA 00:08:20
# Fork: 1 of 5
# Warmup Iteration   1: 100917.316 ops/s
# Warmup Iteration   2: 105528.728 ops/s
# Warmup Iteration   3: 105569.479 ops/s
# Warmup Iteration   4: 104947.364 ops/s
# Warmup Iteration   5: 105071.139 ops/s
Iteration   1: 104137.948 ops/s
                 ·gc.alloc.rate:               1900.765 MB/sec
                 ·gc.alloc.rate.norm:          20120.000 B/op
                 ·gc.churn.G1_Eden_Space:      1906.794 MB/sec
                 ·gc.churn.G1_Eden_Space.norm: 20183.824 B/op
                 ·gc.churn.G1_Old_Gen:         0.002 MB/sec
                 ·gc.churn.G1_Old_Gen.norm:    0.018 B/op
                 ·gc.count:                    33.000 counts
                 ·gc.time:                     22.000 ms

Iteration   2: 104582.692 ops/s
....

# Run complete. Total time: 00:08:22

REMEMBER: The numbers below are just data. To gain reusable insights, you need to follow up on
why the numbers are the way they are. Use profilers (see -prof, -lprof), design factorial
experiments, perform baseline and negative tests that provide experimental control, make sure
the benchmarking environment is safe on JVM/OS/HW level, ask for reviews from the domain experts.
Do not assume the numbers tell you what you want them to tell.

Benchmark                                                                            (controlKeysCount)  (enumsCount)  (prefixesCount)   Mode  Cnt        Score       Error   Units
TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration                                                10          1000                1  thrpt   10     104137.948 ±    31.242   ops/s
TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.alloc.rate                                 10          1000                1  thrpt   10     1900.765 ±    48.356  MB/sec
TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.alloc.rate.norm                            10          1000                1  thrpt   10  20120.000   ±    39.771    B/op
TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.churn.G1_Eden_Space                        10          1000                1  thrpt   10     1906.794 ±    58.341  MB/sec
TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.churn.G1_Eden_Space.norm                   10          1000                1  thrpt   10  20183.824   ± 40835.901    B/op
TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.churn.G1_Old_Gen                           10          1000                1  thrpt   10        0.002 ±     0.027  MB/sec
TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.churn.G1_Old_Gen.norm                      10          1000                1  thrpt   10       0.018 ±    0.012    B/op
TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.count                                      10          1000                1  thrpt   10      33.000              counts
TypeEnumerationAllocationBenchmark.preLoadEnumeration:·gc.time                                       10          1000                1  thrpt   10      22.000                  ms
....

where:

  • Fork - single benchmark execution. Every method executed several times. The number could be changed with -f option. Default value is 5. 0 means no fork and could be useful during benchmark debugging.
  • Warmup Iteration - some benchmark execution for warmup. Warmup results will not be used as benchmark result and requires mainly to eliminate some internal Java compilations and optimizations
  • Iteration # - actual benchmark execution and intermediate results.

More execution options you may find here

Maximillian Arruda - Aug 15

item.author: vbochenin

item.title: 每个软件工程师都应该知道的关于神经多样性的事情 | Things Every Software Engineer Should Know About Neurodiversity
item.description:
哈哈

发表于神经型- 它指的是任何非自闭症的人

  • 神经型 - 一种大脑，就一个人如何解释和响应社会线索等而言。
    • 终生怪人，终于弄明白了。
    • 不是医生或医疗专业人员。
    • 生物多样性的一个子集。
    • 不同的人可以有不同的神经类型的想法。
    • 大多数人的认知和感觉设备的工作方式或多或少与其他人的相同——我们称这些人为神经型。
      • 其他人以不同的方式体验现实的各个方面——我们称这些人为神经发散者，医生为各种情况命名。
    • 自闭症很难定义，似乎是其他几种导致行为和发育差异的条件交叉的结果。
      • 将其视为人类操作系统的一系列可能配置。
      • 很难定义，因为没有两个自闭症患者具有完全相同的特征。
      • 注意：谈话侧重于特征而不是诊断。
    • 大脑的奖励中心受到好奇心而非目标的刺激。
    • 关注那些不“有趣”的事情需要持续的努力。
    • 通常（但不总是）包括难以调节强烈的情绪。
    • 躁郁症患者的情绪波动很大，难以调节。 有各种各样的介绍和治疗。
      • 曾经被称为“躁狂抑郁症”
      • 类型 1：轻躁狂和抑郁交替出现。
      • 在某些人中，症状是由吸毒引发的。
      • 药物存在，但需要权衡取舍。
    • 如果你的大脑处理视觉数据的速度比你的眼睛可以传达的更快，那么阅读和解析视觉细节等事情可能会是一个挑战。
    • 大脑可以以不同的方式处理不同类型的信息。
    • 如果没有写作和数学这些技术，这些诊断就不存在。
      • 残疾是怎么说的？
    • 生活中的广泛连锁效应。
    • 患有强迫症的人正以惊人的规律性经历认识论危机。 这通常需要绝对确定某事。
    • 不是每个人都与外部世界有相同的关系。
    • 感官处理是我们理解现实的方式。
    • 具有不同感官需求的人实际上居住在不同的现实中，我们必须尊重这一点。
    • 强迫症患者正以惊人的规律性经历认识论危机。 这通常需要绝对确定某事。
      • 不是开玩笑-这可能会消耗一切，并且余生几乎没有空间。
      • 行为成分是认知的纯 O变体。 难以诊断常常使患者感到羞耻。
      • 关键是打断大脑的奖励循环，但这很难。
    • 创伤后应激障碍 (PTSD) 发生在您经历过情绪上如此痛苦以至于您无法处理的事情时。
      • 安排你的整个生活，不要去想触发器。
      • 现在你有“闪回”，重温你最糟糕的经历。
    • 复杂的创伤后应激障碍是经历了持续的创伤期的结果。 这是一种普遍存在的状况，影响着生活的方方面面。
    • 战斗/飞行/冻结/讨好反应在威胁下无法控制地出现。
    • 一生都在寻找威胁，在恐惧和愤怒的迷雾中与他人建立联系。
    • 常见于某些类型的 ADHD以及计算障碍和自闭症。
    • 时间流逝的主观体验并不映射到时间的外部流逝。
      • 一切都早或晚。
    • 不是性格缺陷——这实际上是无法像 NT 人那样感知时间。
    • 面部感知的认知障碍，其中能够识别熟悉的面孔，包括自己的面孔（自我识别）
      • 依靠发型、声音、服装、动作等。
    • 一个人没有功能正常的心灵之眼并且不能自愿想象图像的情况。
      • 有些人是极其丰富的视觉思想家，会在记忆中搜寻细节。
      • 如果不看，有些人根本无法想象任何东西。
      • 大多数人似乎介于两者之间。
    • 在自闭症和某些 ADHD 中很常见，很难关闭大脑并入睡 - 或者重新打开并醒来。
      • 慢性失眠
      • 延迟睡眠期综合症
      • 慢性疲劳加剧了现有挑战
    • 与多动症和自闭症一样，终生被纠正会导致人们对令人失望的人的意识增强，并对被拒绝感到恐惧。
      • 认为对大多数人来说没什么大不了的事情可能会毁掉对拒绝敏感的人一天或一周的时间。
      • 一个人的自我意识存在于他人之中，因此一个人需要确认和认可，否则一个人的自我就是“错误的”。
      • 这会导致不必要的孤立和疏离。
    • 与 CPTSD 和拒绝敏感性有关，有些人感到羞耻的程度比他们需要的要强烈得多。
      • 羞耻是其他人如何教你他们希望你如何行动。
      • 有点羞耻是恰当的
      • 如果你因为做自己而不断感到羞耻，那是你学到的东西，你可以忘记它。
    • 对各种事物过敏的人可能会变得超负荷，导致可怕和失调（生理调节机制受损）行为。
      • 明亮的灯光、响亮的声音、特定的纹理，甚至强烈的情绪都会让人感到痛苦。
      • 它可能导致崩溃或关闭，这不是发脾气，而是身体试图保护自己。
      • 处理起来既痛苦又尴尬。
      • 不要取笑波诺的太阳镜。
    • 神经发散者尽最大努力成为和“通过”为神经典型的过程。
      • 每个人都做一点，这就是“适应”的工作方式。
      • 对于神经典型的人来说，就是要避免社会后果。
      • 挑战随神经发散的程度而变化。
      • 这可能会摧毁灵魂，可能会混淆自我。
    • 许多自闭症患者在理解、被理解和寻求接受方面挣扎。
      • 对自闭症的头脑和对全能的头脑来说，不同的事情是显而易见的。
      • 注意不同的细节
      • 因此，我们并不总是用同样的词谈论同样的事情。
    • 性别是一种社会结构，许多神经发散的人（尤其是自闭症患者）努力与神经典型的人分享社会结构。
      • 大量神经发散的人也是性别酷儿。
      • 主观体验与社会交流和期望之间的差异。
      • 从学校环境转移到专业环境是一种文化冲击，自闭症患者的记录很少。
        • 很多东西要学，很多痛苦的试错。
        • 将感觉和声誉置于正确性之上。
        • 听不懂“恭维三明治”？
        • 哦，我的上帝，为什么没有人能理解我想说的这么明显。
      • 从你自己开始。
        • 科学和医学无法获得主观经验。
        • 没有人可以“证明”他们的感受，所以我们必须倾听并相信。
        • 尤其是孩子——当我们的童年需求得不到满足时，会在以后的生活中造成如此多的伤害。
      • 相信你的孩子，当他们告诉你，当他们无法交到朋友时，他们告诉你他们在与质地斗争时会感到痛苦。
        • 早期支持是蓬勃发展与花费数年或数十年时间忘记有毒的应对技能之间的区别。
      • 还; 从你自己开始。
        • 没有人可以成为其他人——这并不意味着不存在自我提升，但重要的是要了解什么可以改进，什么是一个人的内在部分。
      • 尝试在人们所在的地方与他们会面，而不是期望他们在您所在的地方与您会面。
      • 明白许多残疾是无形的，你不知道你的同事在处理什么。
      • 还; 从你自己开始。
        • 多迷走神经理论用一些令人信服的证据证明，除非我们感到安全，否则我们不能成为最完整或最好的自己。

    一旦暂停，nothanii 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，nothanii 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，nothanii 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 nothanii 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。
